resources.string: We have changed our app\'s name from PixelPlay to PixelPlayer due to a trademark-related issue. Keep playing!
Couldn\'t find lyrics for this song.
Control your music from the home screen.

To edit the metadata of your songs (.mp3 files), PixelPlayer needs special permission to access all files. This will allow us to modify the tags of your tracks directly. Please grant this permission on the next screen to enable metadata editing.
Would you like to search for lyrics online?
We couldn’t find lyrics automatically. You can edit the title or artist and try searching manually.
Failed to fetch lyrics from remote
Connection timed out. Please check your internet connection.
Network error. Please check your internet connection.
Server error (code %d). Please try again later.
Embedded lyrics already found. Online fetch skipped.
Always open the picker instead of auto-applying the first match
Unable to open that audio file.
Please configure your Gemini API Key in Settings.
AI couldn\'t find any songs for your prompt.
Write an idea for your Daily Mix
AI couldn\'t find songs for this mix
No valid songs found to play
Let\'s get everything set up for you.
All folders are scanned by default. Pick any locations you want to ignore when building your library.
PixelPlayer needs access to your audio files to build your music library.
Enable notifications to control your music from the lock screen and notification shade.
To ensure the Sleep Timer works reliably and pauses music exactly when you want, PixelPlayer needs permission to schedule exact alarms.
For some Android versions, PixelPlayer needs broader file access to find all your music.
Choose your preferred way to navigate your library.
You can change this later in Settings > Appearance > Library Navigation.
Some Android devices aggressively kill background apps. Disable battery optimization for PixelPlayer to prevent unexpected playback interruptions.
You\'re ready to enjoy your music.
Choose the style of the bottom navigation bar.
You can change this later in Settings > Appearance > Navbar Style.
This huge update brings a complete UI overhaul, 10-band Equalizer, and AI powers.
Material 3 Expressive UI refresh across Settings, Player, and Bottom Sheets.
New 10-band Equalizer with effects, plus AI integration for smarter playlists.
Rebuilt Library Sync for reliability and linear progress reporting.
Major fixes for Casting, Queue stability, and lyrics synchronization.
Share steps to reproduce, what you expected, what happened, and your device/OS. A quick clip or screenshot helps a ton.

string-array.name: changelog_050_highlights
string-array.item: Implemented a 10-band equalizer and effects suite (Feature: @theovilardo)
Added M3U playlist import/export support (Feature/Fix: @lostf1sh, @theovilardo)
Integrated Deezer API for artist images (Feature: @lostf1sh)
Added Gemini AI model selection, system prompt settings, and AI playlist entry (Feature: @lostf1sh, @theovilardo)
Added lyrics sync offset support and multi-strategy remote search (Feature/Fix: @lostf1sh, @theovilardo)
Added baseline profile for performance improvements (Feature/Fix: @theovilardo, @google-labs-julesbot)
Added custom playlist cover support

string-array.name: changelog_050_improvements
string-array.item: Material 3 Expressive UI: modernized settings, stats, player, bottom sheets, and dialogs (Refactor: @theovilardo, @lostf1sh)
Library sync: rebuilt the initial sync flow with phased progress reporting and a linear indicator (Feature: @lostf1sh)
Settings architecture: introduced categorized subscreens and improved navigation handling (Refactor/Fix: @theovilardo)
Queue and player: decoupled queue updates and scroll animations, added animated queue scrolling (Feature/Fix: @lostf1sh, @theovilardo)
Improved widget preview and case-insensitive sorting logic (Feature/Fix: @lostf1sh, @google-labs-julesbot)

string-array.name: changelog_050_fixes
string-array.item: Fixed casting stability, queue transitions, and reduced latency (Fix: @theovilardo)
Fixed delayed content rendering and unexpected collapsing in the player sheet (Fix/Refactor: @theovilardo)
Fixed reordering issues in the queue
General crash fixes and minor UX improvements (Fix: @lostf1sh, @theovilardo)

string-array.name: changelog_040_highlights
string-array.item: Main navigation redesign
New file browser for picking source directories
Landscape mode (thanks "leave this blank for now")
New connect and casting features
Seamless continuity between remote devices
Seamless transitions between songs
Crossfade
New custom transitions (playlist-only)
Keep playing after closing the app
UI improvements
Improved stats
Redesigned queue controls with more features
Improved playback and metadata editing for different file types
Improved permission controller
Minor bug fixes

string-array.name: changelog_030_whats_new
string-array.item: Added a richer listening stats hub to dive into your sessions.
Introduced a floating quick player to open and preview local files instantly.
Added a Folders tab with tree navigation and playlist-ready view.

string-array.name: changelog_030_improvements
string-array.item: Refined the overall Material 3 UI for a cleaner, more cohesive experience.
Smoothed animations and transitions across the app for fluid navigation.
Enhanced artist screen layout with richer details and polish.
Upgraded Daily Mix and Your Mix generation for smarter, more varied picks.
Strengthened the AI assistant for more relevant play suggestions.
Improved search relevance and presentation for faster discovery.
Expanded support for a wider range of audio formats.

string-array.name: changelog_030_fixes
string-array.item: Resolved metadata issues so song details stay accurate everywhere.
Restored notification shortcuts to reliably jump back to playback.

string-array.name: changelog_020_added
string-array.item: Chromecast support to cast audio from your device (temporarily disabled).
In-app changelog to keep you up to date with new features.
Improved lyrics search.
Support for .LRC files, including embedded and external files.
Offline lyrics support.
Synced lyrics (time-aligned with songs).
New screen to view the full queue.
Reorder and remove songs from the queue.
Mini player gestures (swipe down to close).
Added more Material animations.
New settings to customize the look and feel.
New settings to clear cache.

string-array.name: changelog_020_changed
string-array.item: Fully redesigned user interface.
Fully redesigned player.
Improved library performance.
Faster app startup.
AI now delivers better results.

string-array.name: changelog_020_fixed
string-array.item: Fixed various bugs in the tag editor.
Fixed playback notifications not clearing.
Fixed several crashes.

resources.string: How your Daily Mix is created
Your Daily Mix is created from your favorite songs and the ones you listen to most. We also add songs from artists and genres you like so you can discover new music.
Tell the AI what you want to hear today
We use a small sample to keep costs low
Describe the vibe, mood, or activity and let AI curate the perfect playlist from your library.
e.g. Chill evening vibes, upbeat workout energy...
Select the fields you want to generate:
Allow PixelPlayer to see your nearby devices and current Wi‑Fi so we can keep your cast, Bluetooth audio, and speakers in sync.
Needed to read and control your connected Bluetooth audio gear.
Android requires Location to share the Wi‑Fi network (SSID) you\'re on so we can find compatible cast devices.
We only use these permissions for device interconnectivity — casting, controlling nearby speakers, and keeping audio in sync.
Turn on Wi‑Fi or Bluetooth to discover nearby devices
Make sure your TV or speaker is on and sharing the same Wi‑Fi network.
The app crashed during your last session. Help us fix this by sharing the crash report.
Are you sure you want to reset the lyrics for this song?
Manage folders, refresh library, parsing options
Audio behavior, crossfade, and background play
Export and recover your personal app data
Are you sure you want to reset the tab order to the default?
SD card is not available right now.
Jump directly to any tab or reorder them.
Enter a name for your custom equalizer preset.
No songs found in your library.
Try rescanning your library in settings if you have music on your device.
Touch the heart icon in the player to add songs.
This will completely rebuild your music library from scratch. All imported lyrics, favorites, and custom metadata will be lost. This action cannot be undone.
This will discard the current mix and generate a new one based on recent listening habits.
This will clear the statistics cache and force a recalculation from the database history.
Choose exactly what you want to include in the backup package.
Folders here will be skipped when scanning your library.
Automatically scan and assign .lrc files in the same folder during library sync.
Choose which source to try first when fetching lyrics.
Remove all imported lyrics from the database.
Switch between light, dark, or follow system appearance.
Use complex shaped corners effectively improving aesthetics but may affect performance on low-end devices.
Choose the appearance for the floating player.
Show codec, bitrate, and sample rate in the player progress section.
Current: %1$s. Open live preview and choose style.
Choose the appearance for the album carousel.
Choose the appearance for the navigation bar.
Adjust the corner radius of the navigation bar.
Match the navbar shape\'s corners with your device\'s physical corners for a seamless look.
Choose how to move between Library tabs.
If off, removing the app from recents will stop playback.
Disable battery optimization to prevent playback interruptions.
Start playing immediately after switching cast connections.
Remember shuffle setting even after closing the app.
Show previously played songs in the queue.
Normalize volume levels using ReplayGain metadata from audio files.
Track: normalize each song. Album: normalize per album.
In Folders tab, system back navigates folder stack before leaving Library.
Tap the blurred background to close the player sheet.
Enable vibration feedback across the app.
Choose sections, export a .pxpl file, and import it later. Restore only replaces the sections you select.
Browse or pick from recent backups. Selected data will replace current data.
Select a .pxpl backup file to inspect. You\'ll choose which sections to restore in the next step.
Previously imported backups will appear here.
Player UI loading experiments and toggles.
Launch the onboarding setup screen for testing.
Re-creates the daily mix playlist immediately.
Clears cache and recalculates playback statistics.
No songs with album art were found.
Pick a song to rebuild all album color variants from scratch.
Simulate a crash to test the crash reporting system.
Choose sections, export a .pxpl file and import it later. Restore only replaces the sections you select.
Your custom playlists and ordering preferences.
Themes, behavior, playback, and app preferences.
Recent search terms in the app.
Play count and listening duration per song.
Cached artist image URLs from Deezer.
Custom equalizer presets and pinned order.
Edit the metadata for this song.
Are you sure you want to clear all songs from the queue except the current one?
Enter a name for the merged playlist:
This will merge %1$d selected playlists into one.
Scan entire library for new and modified files.
Automatically fetch lyrics for all songs using lrclib.
Preview of the new cover art
Select a square image and fine-tune it so your cover art looks great across the app.
Unable to load the selected image
Touch the \'New Playlist\' button to start.
This folder does not contain songs.
Are you sure you want to delete this playlist?
Design artwork, icon, shape and pick songs yourself.
Generate a curated playlist with advanced controls.
Requires Gemini API key configured in settings.
What should this playlist feel like?
Example: sunset drive with warm synths
Add at least one instruction for AI.
Your final prompt will appear here once you add preferences.
Define mood, activity, constraints and depth.
The AI will only use songs from your local library.
Set your Gemini API key first
Choose which source to try first when fetching lyrics.
Remove all imported lyrics from the database.
Switch between light, dark, or follow system appearance.
Choose the appearance for the floating player.
Show codec, bitrate, and sample rate in the player progress section.
Current: %s. Open live preview and choose style.
Choose the appearance for the album carousel.
Choose the appearance for the navigation bar.
Adjust the corner radius of the navigation bar.
Choose how to move between Library tabs.
If off, removing the app from recents will stop playback.
Start playing immediately after switching cast connections.
In Folders tab, system back navigates folder stack before leaving Library.
Tap the blurred background to close the player sheet.
Select the Gemini model to use.
Select the AI service provider to use.
Select the DeepSeek model to use.
Show collaboration albums under main artist
Artist settings have changed. Rescan your library to apply.

PixelPlayer automatically splits artist tags containing multiple artists. This is useful for songs downloaded with yt-dlp or other tools that use delimiters like \'/\' to separate artists.\n\nBackslash (\\) can be used to escape delimiters.
This app was built with passion and a love for music. We hope you enjoy the experience.
Open source music player built with its community.
Recognition for collaborators with major impact.
Building PixelPlayer with direct community feedback.
Has contributed enormously across core features, architecture and reliability.
Developer of Rhythm (another music app) and key community supporter.
Helped shape PixelPlayer in the first stages of the app.
No contributors found right now. Please try again later.
Choose sections, export a .pxpl file, and import it later. Restore only replaces the sections you select.
Change the range or play more songs to fill this timeline.
Launch the onboarding setup screen for testing.
Re-creates the daily mix playlist immediately.
Clears cache and recalculates playback statistics.
No songs with album art were found.
Pick a song to rebuild all album color variants from scratch.
Select a .pxpl backup file to inspect. You\'ll choose which sections to restore in the next step.
Select a song to clear cached theme data and regenerate all palette styles from the album art.
Search by title, artist, or album
Restore data from a previous backup file.
We will surface your listening habits once we know you better.
Compare how you listen across genres, artists, albums, and songs.
Keep listening and your favorite artists will show up here.
Albums you revisit often will appear here.
Most played tracks for the selected time range.
Listen to your favorites to see them highlighted here.
How your listening time is distributed across your top tracks.
Play more tracks to see how focused your listening is.
Top 3 tracks account for %d%% of your listening time.
Press play to start building your listening timeline
Press play to surface your listening highlights
Total listening captured in the selected range.
How many sessions you completed per segment.
Average listening length for each segment.
Split into 4-hour windows to reveal your daily rhythm.
Daily bars make week-to-week habits easy to compare.
Weekly bars show how the month is trending.
Monthly bars show seasonality across the year.
Yearly bars summarize your full history.
Routes player UI through the new rewrite host. Keep disabled if you notice regressions.
Hold the full player content until the sheet background is fully expanded.
Delay album art and carousel until the sheet is expanded.
Delay title, artist, and lyrics/queue actions.
Delay the timeline and time labels until expansion completes.
Delay play/pause, seek, and favorite controls.
Choose how expanded the sheet must be before delayed components become visible.
Choose how much the sheet must collapse before placeholders take over while closing.
Keep layout stable by rendering lightweight placeholders while components wait for expansion.
Show delayed placeholders immediately when the player starts collapsing.
Placeholders keep their layout space but become invisible.
Step 1: All elements are currently delayed until the player sheet reaches your threshold.
Decide if elements appear based on expansion percentage or only after you let go of the sheet.
Uses spring animations and visual effects for lyrics. May cause frame drops on low-end devices.
Applies a depth-of-field blur to inactive lyrics.
Adjust the intensity of the blur effect.
Enable at least one delay option above to unlock trigger settings.
Trigger mode set to Drag Release. Content will appear only after you release the drag gesture.
Full controls with shuffle and repeat
Manage linked providers and keep each integration under your control.
Connect a provider to manage it from this screen.
Unable to open this screen right now.
Stream music from your cloud accounts
Netease Cloud Music - Sign in to stream
Tap a delimiter to remove it. At least one delimiter is required.
At least one delimiter is required
Delimiter already exists or is invalid
Audio specs, codecs, and decoder info
These songs will be permanently deleted from your device and cannot be recovered.
\"%1$s\" by %2$s\n\nThis song will be permanently deleted from your device and cannot be recovered.
Can\'t delete the file or file not found
Configure default behavior for this specific playlist.
This configuration applies to all playback sources unless overridden.
Enable to set specific rules for this playlist.
This will restore the default preset order and visibility. Continue?
Drag to reorder • Tap eye to show/hide
Everything is allowed by default. Tap a folder to mark it as excluded from scans.
Add music to your device or sync a cloud source to start listening.
Try another source filter or rescan your device library.
Sync Telegram or Netease songs, or switch to local source.
Albums will appear here as soon as your library has grouped tracks.
Local songs are required to build local album groups.
Cloud songs with album data will appear here after sync.
Artists are shown after songs are indexed from any source.
No artist metadata is available for local songs right now.
Cloud artist entries appear when remote songs are synced.
Tap the heart icon while playing a song to save it here.
Switch source filter or like songs from your device.
Like Telegram or Netease tracks to see them in this view.
Internal storage folders with music will appear here.
Create your first playlist to organize your library.
Fetch latest songs from this channel
Stop syncing and remove cached songs
Add public Telegram channels to sync your music library
Tap sync to fetch your playlists
Please grant all required permissions to continue
Try a different search term or check your filters.
Choose the shape arrangement for the Your Mix collage.
Cycle through collage patterns each time you visit Home.
All files access is required to browse folders.
Set your Gemini API key first
Enter a name for the merged playlist:
This will merge %d selected playlists into one.
Choose the album colors for the player UI.
This will remove all your recent search terms. This action cannot be undone.
Your custom playlists and ordering preferences.
Themes, behavior, playback, and app preferences.
Recent search terms in the app.
Play count and listening duration per song.
Cached artist image URLs from Deezer.
Custom equalizer presets and pinned order.
Login with robust error handling, timeout control, and editable steps.
You are editing your number. Sending code again will replace the previous one.
Edit your phone number and send a new verification code.
Please wait for the current request to finish.
Enter the verification code sent by Telegram.
Enter your Telegram two-step verification password.
Telegram request timed out. Check your connection and retry.
Invalid phone number. Confirm country code and number.
Too many attempts. Wait a few minutes and try again.
Invalid verification code. Check it and try again.
Verification code expired. Request a new code.
Session closed. Re-open login to continue.
Enter a name for your custom equalizer preset.
This effect is not supported on your device
Enter your Telegram number. You can come back and edit it later.
Enter the code from Telegram. If the number is wrong, go back and edit it.
Enter your Telegram password. You can still go back to fix your number.
